subject: Switch to i2c-gpio driver for smbus compatibility.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: RTC devices (m41t81@0x68, mcp7941x@0x6F, optional pcf8563@0x51) would not be enumerated on i2c bus; time/date functionality via add-on RTC board would fail silently.

notes:
CRITICAL ESCALATION TRIGGER: This commit is classified in patch-provenance.md:310 as part of a GROUPED row containing 13 commits (aa8afe109 through 077c2c317). The worker instructions §4 explicitly flag grouped rows as a failure mode this project exists to fix. The commit must be analyzed individually, not as part of the group. The i2c-gpio driver is upstreamed in vanilla 6.18.38 (drivers/i2c/busses/i2c-gpio.c present, CONFIG_I2C_GPIO=y enabled in our config). However, vanilla 6.18.38's socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts is minimal (only i2c0+adxl345 accelerometer, mmc0, uart0, gmac1, gpio banks 0-1-2) and does NOT include the RTC bus configuration. The commit transforms the DTS from a previous &i2c1-based approach to i2c-gpio bit-banging on portb 22/23, adding an #include for gpio bindings and removing the ltcmode gpio-leds node. This is MiSTer-specific board support, not upstreamable as-is (mainline has no MiSTer_fb, no MiSTer,spi-audio, no overclock driver). Carried forward by Michael C. Ferguson as 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (P1.7, 2026-07-12) on top of mainline's 6.18 de10nano DTS, re-implemented due to path change (arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/ in 6.18 vs arch/arm/boot/dts/ in 5.15). Stock MiSTer DTB decompiles (docs/stock-inventory/stock.dts) to show identical i2c_gpio configuration with three RTC candidates (pcf8563@0x51, m41t81@0x68, mcp7941x@0x6F), confirming this was stock's actual runtime behavior. No userspace coupling detected: Main_MiSTer's RTC code (user_io.cpp:send_rtc) sends time via FPGA SPI, not Linux i2c; the i2c RTCs are accessed only by kernel drivers, not by userspace. No user-visible effect if RTC add-on board is not present (silent failure). ESCALATION REQUIRED: (1) Commit is in a grouped provenance row; (2) patch-provenance.md claims this group 'landed (P1.7)' but grouping violates the reconciliation contract.

sonnet-verified: carry-mapping CONFIRMED. Re-read the original fork commit diff (`git show 1337de1fd`) and confirmed it matches the record's description exactly: adds #include <dt-bindings/gpio/gpio.h>, comments out the ltcmode gpio-leds node, adds a new i2c_gpio node (compatible="i2c-gpio", gpios on portb 22/23, i2c-gpio,delay-us=<2>) containing the two RTC children moved from the deleted &i2c1 override. 0004's i2c_gpio node (patch lines 161-184) matches this shape exactly, same portb 22/23 gpios, same delay-us=2, plus the pcf8563 child added by a later commit (6827e764). CONFIG_I2C_GPIO=y confirmed set in board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:260, and drivers/i2c/busses/i2c-gpio.c exists in vanilla 6.18. Main_MiSTer citation spot-checked: user_io.cpp:1062 `static void send_rtc(int type)` exists as claimed. No corrections needed.
